mundo.titulo: Signs grow that Starmer will resign as government mood shifts
mundo.resumen: The BBC's Henry Zeffman and Nick Eardley on the mood in government as speculation grows about the prime minister's departure.

mundo.titulo: Extreme heat warning extended to four days as 38C forecast in UK
mundo.resumen: A Met Office amber weather warning for heat comes into force on Monday and will now last through to Thursday.

mundo.titulo: William marks 44th birthday as royals celebrate Father's Day
mundo.resumen: The Princess of Wales and the couple's children share message for "the best papa in the world".

mundo.titulo: US and Iran begin talks on initial peace deal in Switzerland
mundo.resumen: The face-to-face meeting follows ongoing fighting in Lebanon and Iran's claim to have shut the Strait of Hormuz.

mundo.titulo: Disruption to last a week after fatal Bedford train crash
mundo.resumen: Police say 28 people remain in hospital after two trains collided on Friday.

mundo.titulo: Stokes to return as captain for third Test, coach McCullum says
mundo.resumen: England head coach Brendon McCullum says he is ready to work with returning captain Ben Stokes for the deciding Test against New Zealand.

negocios.titulo: Plans to end gazumping with binding agreements in house sales shake-up
negocios.resumen: Sales agreements will be legally binding sooner and making sellers provide more home information up front are part of the planned changes.

negocios.titulo: O'Leary extends Ryanair contract in deal that could net him over £130m
negocios.resumen: The Ryanair boss extends his contract to 2032, in a deal featuring a bonus scheme that could earn him more than €150m (£130m).

negocios.titulo: Warning over 'fragile' public finances as borrowing rises
negocios.resumen: The UK borrowed £23.3bn in May, according to official figures, up almost a third on the same month last year.

negocios.titulo: 'I'd be put off if he asked to split it': Who should pay on a first date?
negocios.resumen: Some insist on splitting the bill, others say the asker should pay, while many still see a man paying as romantic.

negocios.titulo: Brexit cost 6% of UK economy, Bank of England company data suggests
negocios.resumen: Analysis showed how much the UK could have grown if it had not exited the EU.

negocios.titulo: The artificial ice pyramids saving India's mountain villages
negocios.resumen: Himalayan villages are creating artificial glaciers to guarantee water for their crops in the spring.

tecnologia.titulo: Signal’s Meredith Whittaker wants you to remember that AI chatbots ‘are not your friends’
tecnologia.resumen: "These are not your friends. These are not conscious beings. These are not sentient interlocutors.”

tecnologia.resumen: So ... what's your In the Weights score?

tecnologia.titulo: Founders Fund’s outlier bet on humanely killed fish
tecnologia.resumen: Shinkei makes a refrigerator-sized robot called Poseidon to kill fish quickly and humanely.

tecnologia.titulo: Nobel laureate John Jumper is leaving DeepMind for rival Anthropic
tecnologia.resumen: Jumper isn't the only big name leaving Google DeepMind.

tecnologia.titulo: Every new iOS 27 feature that’s worth knowing about
tecnologia.resumen: While it's not flashy like Apple’s new Siri AI and Apple Intelligence upgrades, there are still a number of additions to iOS 27 worth looking at.

tecnologia.titulo: He made your free video player run smoothly. Now he’s doing that for robots.
tecnologia.resumen: French serial entrepreneur and open-source legend Jean-Baptiste Kempf has been building Kyber, an infrastructure layer to control remote devices in real time.

tecnologia.titulo: How Roomba started a robot revolution
tecnologia.resumen: If you had a Roomba, especially in the early days of the robot vacuum, it was in many ways a fairly unsophisticated machine. tecnologia.titulo: Sony’s Xperia 1 VIII is still a phone for the fans
tecnologia.resumen: The Xperia 1 VIII marks an attempt at a step change for Sony's flagship phone line. Not only has it had an aesthetic overhaul, but Sony has also revamped the camera system, dropping the continuous optical zoom telephoto that's defined the last four generations of Xperia phone. It's not all different. Sony staples like a […]

tecnologia.titulo: The Atlantic created a searchable database of the music used to train AI
tecnologia.resumen: Atlantic reporter Alex Reisner recently uncovered four datasets of music being used to train AI models and made them fully searchable for the public. Two of the sets are absolutely enormous at 12 million and 9 million tracks. The other two are much smaller, but still represent a significant amount of training data at over […]

tecnologia.titulo: Musician and YouTuber Hainbach on ‘Breath of the Wild’ and Swiss Army Knives
tecnologia.resumen: Stefan Paul Goetsch, better known as Hainbach, is a German experimental composer, artist, and YouTuber who is perhaps most famous for making music with laboratory equipment and scientific instruments. tecnologia.titulo: Moves of the Diamond Hand is an unfinished, irresistibly weird dice-based RPG
tecnologia.resumen: From its opening minutes, Moves of the Diamond Hand is upfront about what it offers: You're going to have a lot of strange conversations, and you're going to roll a lot of dice.
